LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" AND REQUIRES THE DEMURRING PARTY TO FILE A DECLARATION STATING "[T]HE MEANS BY WHICH" THEY MET AND CONFERRED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY SEPTEMBER 8, 2022. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: S. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's (COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA) Demurrer to the Second and Fourth Causes of Action is granted without leave to amend.

A demurrer serves the limited role of testing the legal sufficiency of a complaint. (Donabedian v. Mercury Ins. Co. (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 968, 994.) A defendant may demurrer on the grounds that the pleading does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (CCP Sec. 430.10(e).) "Except as otherwise provided by statute, a public entity is not liable for an injury, whether such injury arises out of an act or omission of the public entity or a public employee or any other person." (CCP Sec. 815(a).)

Plaintiff's First and Third causes of action are based on allegations that Defendant terminated Plaintiff's employment in violation of Labor Code Sec. 432.7. Plaintiff's Second and Fourth causes of action are based on allegations that Defendant termed Plaintiff's employment in violation of public policy. While the First and Third causes of action are statutory claims, the Second and Fourth causes of action are common law claims. (See Tameny v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (1980) 27 Cal.3d 167, 170 ["when an employer's discharge of an employee violates fundamental principles of public policy, the discharged employee may maintain a tort action and recover damages traditionally available in such actions."].) Defendant demurrers only to the common law claims.

Defendant argues that the Second and Fourth causes of action cannot be brought against a public entity because they are based on the common law. California Supreme Court precedent confirms that "Sec. 815 abolishes common law tort liability for public entities." (Miklosy v. Regents of University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 899.) There, the high court ruled that "Sec. 815 bars Tameny actions against public entities." (Id. at 900.)

Plaintiff argues that the 2014 legislative amendment to Labor Code Sec. 432.7, which occurred after Miklosy, expanded a public employer's liability to include the common law tort of wrongful termination. Plaintiff bases this argument on the 2014 amendment's addition of language expanding the scope of Sec. 432.7 to cover convictions judicially dismissed or ordered sealed pursuant to law. (See 2013Cal. Legis. Serv. Ch. 721 (S.B. 530)(West).)

This statutory interpretation is unconvincing. For decades, before both the 2014 amendment and the Miklosy decision, Labor Code Sec. 432.7 has provided a statutory cause of action against public as well as private entities. (See 1990 Cal. Legis. Serv. 769 (West)["432.7.(a) No employer whether a public agency or private individual or corporation shall"].) Plaintiff's reliance on the 2014 amendment's expansion of a statutory cause of action is irrelevant here. The issue is whether the amendment provides for a common law claim against a public entity. It does not. Plaintiff does not identify anywhere in the text of Labor Code Sec. 432.7, or in the legislative history, indicating that the Legislature intended to abrogate Miklosy and subject public entities to these common law claims. Thus, CCP Sec. 815 bars Plaintiff's Second and Fourth causes of action against Defendant. Since the Plaintiff cannot plead the common law claims against the Defendant, the Court will deny leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA'S DEMURRER to 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT:

Defendant's demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" and requires the demurring party to file a declaration stating "[t]he means by which" they met and conferred. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by September 8, 2022.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
